old_claim.note: Computed Tomography (CT) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: PET depends on positron-emitting radionuclides, many of which are produced using particle accelerators or cyclotrons.

source_shape.source_locator: NCBI Bookshelf chapter on PET tracer isotopes, annihilation photons, and imaging workflow.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The chapter explains PET as imaging based on injected positron-emitting tracers and lists short-lived isotopes used for biological imaging.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports accelerator/radionuclide production context as the relevant enabler and rejects CT as a prerequisite for PET unless the node is explicitly PET/CT.

ontology_before: The PET node depended on CT, conflating standalone PET with later combined PET/CT scanner systems.
ontology_after: PET now depends on nuclear medicine, positron-emitting radionuclide production context, and signal/image reconstruction.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: PET no longer depends directly on CT. Preserved: PET remains connected to medical imaging reconstruction and nuclear medicine.
would_reject_if: The node were renamed or scoped to PET/CT rather than standalone PET.
A source showed PET requires CT as a method prerequisite rather than as a later hybrid modality.
The graph removed nuclear medicine or radionuclide context from PET.
short_reason: Standalone PET is a radiotracer nuclear-medicine modality, not a CT-dependent modality.
